$comment: PIPELINE GUARD - the FLOOR, not a cage. Added 2026-07-14 when the scheduled agents were set to
'skip all approvals'. That was the right call (a headless agent that hits an approval prompt hangs
forever) but it removed the last interactive backstop, and every other safety rule we had was PROSE.
DENY-ONLY BY DESIGN. There is deliberately NO 'ask' rule anywhere in this file: an ask-prompt in a
headless run hangs the queue forever. That is why the first version of this config was thrown away
before it ever ran - it also denied git inside C:\po-watcher, where the watcher's own agents WORK,
and would have bricked the queue on the first run.
Agents keep full git, full gh, full filesystem. The hook blocks only three things:
1. Azure / Entra / SharePoint tenant writes (absolute - shared company infrastructure)
2. Merging a PR on the NEVER-MERGE list (read from pipeline-lib.ps1 - one source of truth)
3. Force-push / deletion of `main` (force-pushing a FEATURE branch stays allowed - rebases need it)
Tested: .claude/hooks/test-guard.mjs - 34/34, including every command the watcher runs daily.
The hook FAILS OPEN on its own bugs: a broken guard that blocks everything is an outage.
